concepts.title: Foundation of Vijayanagara Empire
concepts.description: The Vijayanagara Empire was founded by Harihara and Bukka Raya I in 1336 AD, marking the beginning of a new era in South Indian history.

concepts.title: Location of Vijayanagara
concepts.description: Vijayanagara was located on the Deccan Plateau, near the modern-day city of Hampi in Karnataka.

concepts.title: Social and Economic Life
concepts.description: The Vijayanagara Empire was known for its rich social and economic life, with a strong emphasis on agriculture, trade, and craftsmanship. The empire was also a centre of learning and art, with many temples and educational institutions.

concepts.title: Key Features of Vijayanagara Architecture
concepts.description: Use the acronym 'VIBGYOR' to remember the key features of Vijayanagara architecture: V - Vaults, I - Imitation of Dravidian style, B - Buddhist influence, G - Granite temples, Y - Yogic symbols, O - Ornate carvings, R - Rajput influence

concepts.title: Successors of Harihara
concepts.description: Students often confuse the successors of Harihara. To avoid this, remember that Harihara's successors were Bukka Raya I, Devaraya I, and Devaraya II, with Devaraya I being the most notable ruler of the empire.

concepts.title: Timeline of Vijayanagara Empire
concepts.description: Remember the timeline of the Vijayanagara Empire: 1336 - Founding of the empire, 1342 - Harihara's coronation, 1350 - Bukka Raya I's coronation, 1404 - Devaraya I's coronation, 1446 - Tuluva period, 1485 - Saluva period, 1509 - Aravidu period, 1565 - Fall of the empire

concepts.title: Economic Activities
concepts.description: The Vijayanagara Empire was a major centre of trade and commerce, with a strong emphasis on agriculture, textile production, and metalwork.

concepts.title: Vijayanagara's Relationship with Neighbouring Kingdoms
concepts.description: Vijayanagara maintained a complex relationship with its neighbouring kingdoms, including the Bahmani Sultanate and the Gajapati Kingdom, often engaging in wars and alliances to maintain its power and influence.

concepts.title: Cultural Achievements
concepts.description: The Vijayanagara Empire was known for its rich cultural achievements, including the development of Kannada literature, the composition of the Puranas, and the creation of intricate temple carvings and sculptures.
